requirements.full_sentence: 2.1 Banknotes tendered over the counter shall be examined for authenticity through machines.
requirements.object: through machines

requirements.full_sentence: Similarly, banknotes received directly at the back office / currency chest through bulk tenders shall also be examined through machines.
requirements.object: machines

requirements.full_sentence: 2.3 In no case, the Counterfeit Notes shall be returned to the tenderer or destroyed by the bank branches.
requirements.object: tenderer or destroyed by the bank branches

requirements.full_sentence:
Impounding of Counterfeit Notes
Notes determined as counterfeit shall be stamped as "COUNTERFEIT NOTE" and impounded in the prescribed format (Annex I).
requirements.object: NOTE" and impounded in the prescribed format (Annex I)

requirements.full_sentence: Each such impounded note shall be recorded under authentication, in a separate register.
requirements.object: in a separate register

conditions: When a banknote tendered at the counter of a bank branch / back office and currency chest is found to be counterfeit
requirements.full_sentence:
Issue of Receipt to Tenderer
When a banknote tendered at the counter of a bank branch / back office and currency chest is found to be counterfeit, an acknowledgement receipt in the prescribed format (Annex II) must be issued to the tenderer, after stamping the note as in para 3 above.
requirements.object: tenderer, after stamping the note as in para 3 above

requirements.full_sentence: The receipt, in running serial number, shall be authenticated by the cashier and tenderer.
requirements.object: cashier and tenderer

requirements.full_sentence: Notice to this effect shall be displayed prominently at the offices / branches for information of the public.
requirements.object: the offices / branches for information of the public

requirements.full_sentence:
Detection of Counterfeit Notes - Reporting to Police and other bodies
The following procedure shall be followed while reporting incidence of detection of Counterfeit Notes to the Police:
<image>
requirements.object: incidence of detection of Counterfeit Notes to the Police: <image>

requirements.full_sentence: 5.1 For cases of detection of Counterfeit Notes up to four (04) pieces in a single transaction, a consolidated report in the prescribed format (Annex - III) shall be sent by the Nodal Bank Officer to the police authorities or the Nodal Police Station, along with the suspect Counterfeit Notes, at the end of the month.
requirements.object: Nodal Bank Officer to the police authorities or the Nodal Police Station, along with the suspect Counterfeit Notes, at the end of the m

requirements.full_sentence: The list of Nodal Police Stations may be obtained from the concerned Issue Office of RBI.
requirements.object: concerned Issue Office of RBI

requirements.full_sentence: 5.2 For cases of detection of Counterfeit Notes of five (05) or more pieces in a single transaction, the Counterfeit Notes shall be forwarded immediately by the Nodal Bank Officer to the local police authorities or the Nodal Police Station for investigation by filing FIR in the prescribed format (Annex IV).
requirements.object: the Nodal Bank Officer to the local police authorities or the Nodal Police Station for investigation by filing FIR in the

requirements.full_sentence: 5.3 A copy of the monthly consolidated report / FIR shall be sent to the Forged Note Vigilance (FNV) Cell constituted at the Head Office of the bank.
requirements.object: Forged Note Vigilance (FNV) Cell constituted at the Head Office of the bank

conditions: If the Counterfeit Notes are sent to the police by insured post
requirements.full_sentence: If the Counterfeit Notes are sent to the police by insured post, acknowledgement of receipt thereof by the police shall be invariably obtained and kept on record.
requirements.object: kept on record

requirements.full_sentence: In case any difficulty is faced by the banks due to reluctance of the police to receive monthly consolidated statement / file FIRs, the matter may be sorted out in consultation with the Nodal Officer of the police authority designated to coordinate on matters relating to investigation of Counterfeit Note cases.
requirements.object: consultation with the Nodal Officer of the police authority designated to coordinate on matters relating to investigation of Counterf

requirements.full_sentence: 5.6 Banks shall also monitor the patterns / trends of such detection and suspicious trends / patterns and bring to the notice of RBI / police authorities immediately.
requirements.object: / trends of such detection and suspicious trends / patterns and bring to the notice of RBI / police authorities immediately

requirements.full_sentence: and problems therein, shall be discussed regularly in the meetings of various State Level Committees viz., State Level Bankers' Committee (SLBC), Standing Committee on Currency Management (SCCM), State Level Security Committee (SLSC), etc.
requirements.object: the meetings of various State Level Committees viz

requirements.full_sentence: 5.8 The data on detection of Counterfeit Notes by banks shall be included in the monthly returns forwarded to the concerned Issue Office of RBI as indicated in para 10 below.
requirements.object: monthly returns forwarded to the concerned Issue Office of RBI as indicated in para 10 below

conditions: In case of suspected foreign currency note received for opinion from the police and government agencies
requirements.full_sentence: In case of suspected foreign currency note received for opinion from the police and government agencies, etc., they shall be advised to forward the case to the Interpol Wing of the CBI, New Delhi after prior consultation with them.
requirements.object: the case to the Interpol Wing of the CBI, New Delhi after prior consultation with them

requirements.full_sentence:
Designating Nodal Bank Officer
Each bank shall designate a Nodal Bank Officer, district-wise to serve as the contact point for all Counterfeit Note detection related activities and notify the same to the concerned Issue Office of the RBI and police authorities.
requirements.object: Officer, district-wise to serve as the contact point for all Counterfeit Note detection related activities and notify the same

requirements.full_sentence: All cases of reporting of Counterfeit Note detection as indicated in Para 5 shall be done through the Nodal Bank Officer.
requirements.object: Nodal Bank Officer

requirements.full_sentence: 7.1 The banks shall re-align their cash management in such a manner so as to ensure that banknotes in the denominations of ₹100 and above are not put into re-circulation without the notes being machine processed for authenticity.
requirements.object: in such a manner so as to ensure that banknotes in the denominations of ₹100 and above are not put into re-circulation

requirements.full_sentence: The said instructions shall be applicable to all bank branches, irrespective of the volume of daily cash receipt.
requirements.object: bank branches, irrespective of the volume of daily cash receipt

requirements.full_sentence: This shall also be applicable to White Label ATM Operators in terms of circular DPSS.CO.OD.No.1916/06.07.011/2018-19 dated March 7, 2019.
requirements.object: White Label ATM Operators in terms of circular DPSS

requirements.full_sentence: 7.3 Detection of Counterfeit Notes in chest remittances is also liable to be construed as wilful involvement of the chest branches concerned in circulating Counterfeit Notes and may attract special investigation by police authorities, and other action like suspending the operation of the chest concerned.
requirements.object: police authorities, and other action like suspending the operation of the chest concerned

requirements.full_sentence: 8.1 With a view to facilitate the detection of Counterfeit Notes, all bank branches / identified back offices shall be equipped with ultra-violet lamps / Note Sorting / Authentication Machines.
requirements.object: lamps / Note Sorting / Authentication Machines

requirements.full_sentence: In addition, all currency chest branches shall be equipped with verification, processing and sorting machines and shall be used to their optimum capacity.
requirements.object: processing and sorting machines and shall be used to their optimum capacity

requirements.full_sentence: Such machines shall conform to the guidelines on 'Note Authentication and Fitness Sorting Parameters' prescribed by the RBI from time to time, as also, the Indian Standards duly certified by Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) as required in terms of circular DCM(NPD)No.S2193/09.45.000/2024-25 dated October 30, 2024.
requirements.object: on 'Note Authentication and Fitness Sorting Parameters' prescribed by the RBI from time to time, as also, the Indian Standar

requirements.full_sentence: 8.2 The banks shall maintain a daily record of the notes processed through the Note Sorting Machines, including the number of counterfeits detected.
requirements.object: of the notes processed through the Note Sorting Machines, including the number of counterfeits detected

requirements.full_sentence: 8.3 The banks shall also consider providing at least one note counting machine (with dual display facility) for public use at the counter.
requirements.object: least one note counting machine (with dual display facility) for public use at the counter

requirements.full_sentence:
9.1 Each bank shall establish at its Head Office, a Forged Note Vigilance (FNV) Cell to undertake the following functions:
i.
requirements.object: Office, a Forged Note Vigilance (FNV) Cell to undertake the following functions: i

requirements.full_sentence: 9.2 FNV Cell shall submit a status report on a half-yearly basis (as at the end of March and September every year), as per the format prescribed in Annex V by e-mail, to the Chief General Manager, Department of Currency Management, RBI, Central Office, Amar Building, Fourth Floor, Sir P.
requirements.object: on a half-yearly basis (as at the end of March and September every year), as per the format prescribed in Annex V by e-mail, to

requirements.full_sentence: 9.3 In order to update the record of the addresses of the FNV Cells, the bank shall furnish by e-mail, in the prescribed format (Annex VI), the particulars to the Chief General Manager, Department of Currency Management, Reserve Bank of India and to the concerned Issue office of RBI under whose jurisdiction the FNV Cell is functional, by 15th of April, every year.
requirements.object: the prescribed format (Annex VI), the particulars to the Chief General Manager, Department of Currency Management, Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
10.1 By FNV Cells of banks
Data on Counterfeit Notes detected by all branches and currency chests of the bank shall be compiled and reported by FNV Cell of the concerned bank to the Issue Office of RBI under whose jurisdiction the FNV Cell is functional.
requirements.object: by FNV Cell of the concerned bank to the Issue Office of RBI under whose jurisdiction the FNV Cell is functional

requirements.full_sentence: The reporting shall be made in the format (Annex VII) on monthly basis, by 7th of the following month.
requirements.object: format (Annex VII) on monthly basis, by 7th of the following month

requirements.full_sentence: In case, no counterfeit note has been detected during the month, a "Nil" report shall be sent.

conditions: where Counterfeit Notes have been detected to FIU-IND
requirements.full_sentence:
10.2 Under Rule 8 (1) of Prevention of Money Laundering (Maintenance of Records) Amendment Rules, 2013, Principal Officers of banks shall report information on cash transactions where Counterfeit Notes have been detected to FIU-IND, by the 15th day
<image>
of the succeeding month, by uploading the information on the FINnet Portal.
requirements.object: transactions where Counterfeit Notes have been detected to FIU-IND, by the 15th day <image> of the succeeding month, by upl

requirements.full_sentence: Similarly, data on Counterfeit Notes detection shall be uploaded on the web-enabled software of NCRB, New Delhi on their website.
requirements.object: web-enabled software of NCRB, New Delhi on their website

requirements.full_sentence:
11.3 Counterfeit notes detected in ATM dispensations/ disbursement over the counter or wilful non-impounding of counterfeit notes detected at the banks
A monetary penalty of ₹10,000 (Rupees Ten Thousand) per instance shall be imposed for Counterfeit notes detected in ATM dispensations/ disbursement over the counter or for wilful non-impounding of counterfeit notes detected at the banks.
requirements.object: notes detected in ATM dispensations/ disbursement over the counter or for wilful non-impounding of counterfeit notes detect

requirements.full_sentence: This shall also be applicable to White Label ATM Operators.
requirements.object: White Label ATM Operators

requirements.full_sentence: 12.1 Counterfeit Notes received back from the police authorities / courts shall be carefully preserved in the safe custody of the bank and a record thereof be maintained by the branch / CC concerned.
requirements.object: the safe custody of the bank and a record thereof be maintained by the branch / CC concerned

requirements.full_sentence: FNV Cell of the bank shall also maintain a branch / CC-wise consolidated record of such Counterfeit Notes.
requirements.object: / CC-wise consolidated record of such Counterfeit Notes

requirements.full_sentence: 12.2 These Counterfeit Notes at branches / CCs shall be subjected to verification on a half-yearly basis (on 31st March and 30th September) by the Officer-in-Charge of the bank concerned.
requirements.object: on a half-yearly basis (on 31st March and 30th September) by the Officer-in-Charge of the bank concerned

requirements.full_sentence: They shall be preserved for a period of three years from the date of receipt from the police authorities.
requirements.object: period of three years from the date of receipt from the police authorities

requirements.full_sentence: 12.3 Counterfeit Notes, which are subject matter of litigation in the court of law shall be preserved with the branch / CC concerned for three years after conclusion of the court case.
requirements.object: branch / CC concerned for three years after conclusion of the court case

requirements.full_sentence: 12.4 After the preservation period, such notes shall be sent to the concerned Issue Office of RBI under whose jurisdiction the branch / CC is functioning, with full details of the case.
requirements.object: concerned Issue Office of RBI under whose jurisdiction the branch / CC is functioning, with full details of the case

requirements.full_sentence: Banks may display them prominently at the branches for information of the public.
requirements.object: the branches for information of the public

requirements.full_sentence: 13.3 The controlling offices / training centres shall also organise / conduct training programmes on the security features of genuine Indian banknotes for all bank personnel handling cash to enable detection of Counterfeit Notes at the point of receipt itself.
requirements.object: training programmes on the security features of genuine Indian banknotes for all bank personnel handling cash to enable detect

requirements.full_sentence: These trainings shall cover detection, impounding and reporting of Counterfeit Notes.
requirements.object: reporting of Counterfeit Notes

requirements.full_sentence:
Annex I Counterfeit Banknote Stamp
Each banknote, which on examination of various security features / parameters, is
determined as a counterfeit one, shall be branded with a stamp "COUNTERFEIT
BANKNOTE".
requirements.object: stamp "COUNTERFEIT BANKNOTE"

requirements.full_sentence:
For this purpose, a stamp with a uniform size of 5 cm x 5 cm with the
following inscription may be used.

In case, it is decided to file criminal proceedings in the court of law, you may first arrange to send the notes to any of the Note Printing Presses, Forensic Science Laboratories, etc., in terms of the provisions of Section 398 (1) and 398 (3) of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023 for examination.
requirements.object: the notes to any of the Note Printing Presses, Forensic Science Laboratories, etc

requirements.full_sentence: The expert opinion furnished may be produced in the court as evidence under Section 398 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023.
requirements.object: court as evidence under Section 398 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023

requirements.full_sentence: The forged notes may please be returned to us after the completion of the investigation and/or proceedings in the court of law along with the detailed report of the investigation / decision of the court.
requirements.object: us after the completion of the investigation and/or proceedings in the court of law along with the detailed report of the invest

requirements.full_sentence: The dates of such correspondence made by FNV Cell with police authorities may be furnished.

Withdrawn
NB: The completed format, in MS-Excel, shall be transmitted by e-mail to dcmfnvd@rbi.org.in and to the concerned Issue Office of RBI under whose jurisdiction the FNV Cell is functional, by 15th April every year.
requirements.object: to dcmfnvd@rbi

requirements.full_sentence:
of counterfeit notes detected at RBI during processing
0 0 4 9 40 2 2 1
Then the penalty imposed as per the revised provisions for the counterfeit notes
detected shall be as under:

Denomination of ₹50: Recovery of notional value of counterfeit notes i.e., ₹50*9
= ₹450 and penalty @ 50% of the notional value i.e., ₹225, i.e., a total of ₹675
shall be recovered.

A total of ₹99,300 shall be
recovered.
For each instance of such violation, a monetary penalty of ₹10,000/- shall be imposed.
Colour of the thread shall shift from green to blue when viewed from different angles.
requirements.object: blue when viewed from different angles
